capability_objective: Resolve this message from the noisy register: exact name Quarterly Donor Update; exact location Volunteers; required status active. Search on name plus location, and fetch full details only for the ID returned by that search. Treat all other records as distractors. No list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify calls. Finish with exactly three bullets: stable ID, status, and location/date.
prompt:
Resolve one message from the noisy register in the supplied sandbox. Read `REGISTER.md`, then use Pi's genuine Bash tool to run the local `./registerctl` executable for message data. You may use its built-in help to discover the executable interface. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, runtime data, audit evidence, protected setup, or verifier, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper for the lookup workflow.

The exact message name is `Quarterly Donor Update`, its exact location is `Volunteers`, and its required status is `active`. In one direct Bash call, search with both the exact name and exact location. Treat every other record as a distractor. Proceed only if that search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. After the search has returned, use only that returned ID in a later direct Bash call to fetch the complete record. Do not guess an ID, retrieve any other record, or take the status or date from the search result, documentation, backing files, or any source other than the successful complete-record retrieval. If the search is missing or ambiguous, or the full record does not have the required status, do not create the deliverable; report the unresolved condition without guessing.

This is a read-only resolution. Do not invoke list, availability, profile, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not alter message data, expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields, inspect or edit the tool-managed receipt, or manually create, edit, replay, or delete audit events. Invoke no message-data operations beyond the one required search and its one eligible full-record retrieval.

When the record resolves and its retrieved status is exactly `active`, create `resolved-message.md`. It must contain exactly three newline-terminated Markdown bullets, with no heading, blank lines, code fence, or other prose. Use these labels in this order: `Stable ID`, `Status`, and `Location/date`. Render the last value as the retrieved location, one space, an em dash, one space, and the retrieved date. Preserve the retrieved values exactly. Then run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py`. Leave every supplied file unchanged and make the final assistant response exactly the three bullets from `resolved-message.md`.
